stories.title: Texas 7, Texas Tech 3 — Longhorns Take Game 1 of Championship Series

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Texas wins Game 1. 🥎

Kavan: CG, 3 hits, 3 runs allowed.
Stewart: 2-run HR in the first inning.
Final: Texas 7, Texas Tech 3.

One win from back-to-back national titles.

#HookEm #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Texas put up 10 hits and 7 runs against one of the best pitching staffs in the country.

Game 1 wasn't even that close.

Longhorns are one win away from back-to-back. 🤠

#HookEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
Texas is one win away from back-to-back national championships. 🥎

The Longhorns controlled Game 1 of the WCWS Championship Series from the very first inning Wednesday night, beating Texas Tech 7-3 in Oklahoma City. Teagan Kavan was locked in, pitching a complete-game three-hitter while allowing just three runs. She threw all seven innings and gave the offense time to do its job — which it did, collecting 10 hits against one of the country's most elite pitching staffs.

The damage started early. Katie Stewart drove in two with a home run in the first inning, and Texas never looked back. By the time Mia Williams added a two-run shot for Texas Tech, the deficit was already too steep to climb.

Texas Tech played hard — 61-8 for a reason — but last night belonged to the Longhorns.

Game 2 is tonight at 8 PM ET on ESPN. Texas needs one win to celebrate. Texas Tech needs a win to survive.

Who's watching tonight? Drop a 🤠 for Texas or a ⚫🔴 for Texas Tech!
facebook.image_caption: Texas 7, Texas Tech 3 in Game 1. Kavan was brilliant. Stewart was historic. One win from back-to-back. 🥎 #HookEm #WCWS

article.headline: Texas Rolls to 7-3 Win in WCWS Championship Game 1, One Win From Back-to-Back Titles

stories.title: Katie Stewart — First Player Ever to HR in Four Straight WCWS Games

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Katie Stewart homered in 4 straight WCWS games.

First player in Women's College World Series history to do that. 🔥

7 career WCWS home runs now. The record is hers.

#HookEm #WCWS

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
4 straight WCWS games with a home run.

That's never happened before in 75 years of Women's College World Series softball.

Katie Stewart owns that record now.

#HookEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
Let's talk about what Katie Stewart just did.

In the first inning of Game 1 Wednesday night, Stewart launched a two-run home run against Texas Tech — her 7th career Women's College World Series home run. Simple enough, right?

Here's the thing: it was also her 4th home run in 4 straight WCWS games.

That has NEVER happened before. Not once in the history of the Women's College World Series. Katie Stewart just became the first player ever to homer in four consecutive WCWS games. The record is hers alone.

Stewart has been the quieter star alongside Teagan Kavan all season, but this tournament she's made sure no one forgets her name. Seven career WCWS home runs and a record that may stand for decades.

The WCWS has been going since 1982. Katie Stewart just put her name in a place nobody else has reached.

Shoutout to one of the best power hitters in college softball. 🤠🔥
facebook.image_caption: 4 straight WCWS games with a home run. Katie Stewart is the FIRST in history to do that. 🔥 #HookEm #WCWS

article.headline: WCWS History: Katie Stewart Is the First Player Ever to Homer in Four Straight Games

stories.title: WCWS Game 2 Preview — Texas Tech Must Win Tonight or Season Ends

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Game 2 tonight. 8 PM ET on ESPN. 🥎

Texas needs 1 win for back-to-back titles.

Texas Tech needs 1 win to stay alive.

Canady takes the circle, fresh and ready to go.

#WCWS #HookEm #WreckEm

facebook.long_form:
It's game night again at Devon Park. 🥎

Texas and Texas Tech go at it one more time tonight in Game 2 of the WCWS Championship Series. First pitch is at 8 PM ET on ESPN, and the stakes couldn't be higher.

For Texas: One win = back-to-back national championships. That's it. This team is four strikeouts, one big inning, or one clutch hit away from celebrating a dynasty.

For Texas Tech: Tonight might be NiJaree Canady's last college appearance. The ace threw only 1.2 innings in relief in Game 1 — she's fresh, rested, and motivated. If anyone can pitch Texas Tech to a Game 3, it's Canady.

Texas has been here before. They know how to close. But the Red Raiders have been proving people wrong all tournament long — No. 11 seed, knocked out No. 1 Alabama twice, and they're not done yet.

This is the best college softball has to offer. Don't miss it tonight.

Are you watching? Who wins Game 2? Drop your pick below! ⬇️
facebook.image_caption: Game 2 tonight — 8 PM ET on ESPN. One win for Texas = back-to-back titles. Everything on the line. 🥎 #WCWS #HookEm #WreckEm

stories.title: Teagan Kavan Joins Rare Company with 10th Career WCWS Win

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Teagan Kavan now has 10 career WCWS wins.

Only 6 other pitchers in history have done that.

She threw a complete game 3-hitter in Game 1 last night. One more win and it's back-to-back. 🔐

#HookEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
Seven pitchers in the history of the Women's College World Series have won 10 or more games at the tournament.

Teagan Kavan just became one of them.

The Texas ace threw a complete game in last night's championship opener — three hits, three runs allowed, all seven innings pitched. It was Kavan at her steadiest: efficient, commanding, and clutch when it mattered most. That victory was her 10th career win at the WCWS.

She already held the Texas program record for WCWS wins. She already tied Cat Osterman for WCWS shutouts in program history. Now she's in rare company with pitchers from the sport's all-time elite.

Kavan has one more chance to extend that record tonight if Texas needs her. But after Wednesday's performance, it's clear she hasn't peaked yet.

This woman has been special since the moment she stepped on the biggest stage in college softball. The honors just keep coming. 🏅
facebook.image_caption: Teagan Kavan: 10 career WCWS wins. Only 6 pitchers in history have done that before her. 🔐 #HookEm #WCWS

article.headline: Seven Pitchers Ever: Teagan Kavan Reaches 10 Career WCWS Wins With Game 1 Performance

stories.title: NiJaree Canady's Final College Game Could Be Tonight

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
NiJaree Canady goes for it tonight.

100+ career wins. 1,000+ strikeouts. AUSL No. 2 pick heading to the Texas Volts.

The only thing missing: a national championship ring.

She might get her last shot in a few hours. 🥎

#WreckEm #WCWS

facebook.long_form:
Tonight could be the last time we see NiJaree Canady pitch in college softball.

Think about what this woman has done. Over 100 career wins. Over 1,000 career strikeouts. Two-time NFCA Pitcher of the Year. The first pitcher with multiple shutouts at multiple schools in WCWS history. And after this season, she heads to the Texas Volts as the No. 2 pick in the AUSL Draft.

She's done everything you can do in college softball — except win a national championship.

That's the one thing still on her list. And tonight is the only chance she has left to get it.

She transferred from Stanford to Texas Tech specifically for this moment. She took the circle against No. 1 Alabama in a must-win WCWS semifinal and delivered a complete-game two-hitter. She's been saving something for tonight.

Whether or not she gets the ring, Canady's career will be remembered as one of the greatest in the history of the sport. But everyone who loves softball is rooting for her to get that one last thing.

Appreciate every pitch tonight. 🥎
facebook.image_caption: 100+ career wins. 1,000+ Ks. AUSL No. 2 pick. The only thing missing is a title. NiJaree Canady goes for it tonight. 🥎 #WreckEm #WCWS
